sql:
SELECT *
FROM orders o
WHERE o.customer_id IN (
    SELECT c.id
    FROM customers c
    WHERE LOWER(c.country) = 'india'
)
AND o.id IN (
    SELECT oi.order_id
    FROM order_items oi
    WHERE oi.product_id IN (
        SELECT p.id
        FROM products p
        WHERE p.category = 'electronics'
    )
)
AND o.id IN (
    SELECT p2.order_id
    FROM payments p2
    WHERE p2.status = 'SUCCESS'
)
AND o.created_at >= '2023-01-01'
ORDER BY o.created_at DESC;
suggested_sql:
SELECT
    o.id,
    o.customer_id,
    o.product_id,
    o.quantity,
    o.status,
    o.ordered_at,
    o.created_at
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c
    ON c.id = o.customer_id
JOIN order_items oi
    ON oi.order_id = o.id
JOIN products p
    ON p.id = oi.product_id
JOIN payments p2
    ON p2.order_id = o.id
WHERE
    c.country = 'india'
    AND p.category = 'electronics'
    AND p2.status = 'SUCCESS'
    AND o.created_at >= '2023-01-01'
ORDER BY o.created_at DESC;
note: Rewrite IN subqueries into JOINs; select explicit columns.
